summary:
The answer choice should reflect that full-range moving joints called diarthrosis allow for greater movements due to their characteristic features such as a synovial capsule filled with fluid facilitating wide-ranging motions across multiple axes unlike more limited or fixed counterparts classified under other categories including synarthritis and amphiartrosis where flexibility ranges are significantly reduced owing primarily from structural differences limiting freedom relative to freely articulating bones within these structures respectively.
The summary highlights how diarthrodial joints provide maximum movement through characteristics unique among different classifications—namely possessing flexible connections enabled via synovial membranes containing lubricants allowing unrestricted multi-directional activity contrasted against lesser degrees available elsewhere depending upon anatomical design specifics dictating lessened capabilities regarding rotational extent etcetera inherent amongst non-diatrhoroidal varieties thus underscoring why option pertaining thereto would be correct when posed query about highest degree
